title: Clip Envelopes
summary: Per-clip automation of volume, pitch, or any device parameter. Unlike Arrangement automation (global), clip envelopes travel with the clip — useful for sounds that vary clip-to-clip.
steps: Select a MIDI or audio clip in Session or Arrangement view
In the clip view (bottom panel), click the 'E' tab (Envelopes) to open Clip Envelopes
Device chooser: select which device's parameter to automate (e.g. 'Clip' for volume/pan, or a specific device like 'Serum')
Control chooser: select the specific parameter (Volume, Transposition, or any device knob)
Draw envelope automation in the envelope lane — breakpoints work the same as Arrangement automation
Linked mode: when linked (default), the envelope loops with the clip. Unlinked: the envelope can have a different loop length from the MIDI notes — useful for evolving textures
tips: Clip envelope volume automation is useful for builds: the same clip loops but the volume ramps up over multiple repetitions in Arrangement view — without separate Arrangement automation on the track
Unlinked clip envelopes are powerful: a 4-bar MIDI loop with a 7-bar volume envelope creates an evolving pattern that never exactly repeats in the same phase relationship
Pitch (Transposition) clip envelope: automate the pitch of a clip for pitch bends or drops without using a pitchbend MIDI message. Pitch -12 over 4 bars = tape slowdown effect
Clip envelopes are clip-specific — different clips on the same track can have completely different envelopes. Arrangement automation is track-wide
practice_hint: In any rep with a chord pad clip, select the clip, go to the Envelopes tab, select Clip > Volume. Draw a fade-in from -inf to 0dB over 8 bars. This clip now always fades in when it plays, regardless of Arrangement automation.
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
